key_points: Area formulas are derived from basic shapes like rectangles
A triangle has half the area of a parallelogram with the same base and height
The height of a parallelogram is the perpendicular distance between its bases
Diagonals of a rhombus divide it into four equal triangles
Irregular polygons can be divided into triangles to calculate area
examples: Area of triangle with base 12 cm and height 10 cm = 60 cm²
Area of parallelogram with base 15 cm and height 8 cm = 120 cm²
Area of rhombus with diagonals 10 cm and 8 cm = 40 cm²
Area of trapezium with parallel sides 8 cm and 12 cm and height 5 cm = 50 cm²
exam_tips: Always check that height is perpendicular to the base
Label base and height clearly in diagrams
Use correct units in final answers
Break complex figures into simpler shapes
